assertions.title: Submit query and see loading state

steps.action: Navigate to http://localhost:3100 and select destrieux engine
steps.expected: App loads with destrieux engine selected
steps.observed: App loaded, destrieux engine highlighted in sidebar

steps.action: Load example query 'Union of atlas regions' by clicking the example button
steps.expected: Multi-rule Datalog query loaded into code editor and React QueryContext state
steps.observed:
Query loaded correctly: LeftRegion(s, r):- destrieux(s, r) & startswith("L", s)
AnteriorOfCentralSulcus... etc. Both CM editor and React state confirmed synced.

steps.action: Intercept WebSocket with 3s delay, then click 'Run Query'
steps.expected: Button changes to 'Running...' spinner with Cancel button visible during execution
steps.observed: Button shows '◌ Running...' with disabled state AND a red '✗ Cancel' button next to it, clearly visible at 800ms after click

evidence.network: WebSocket ws://localhost:3100/v1/statementsocket - intercepted with 3s artificial delay to capture loading state

assertions.title: Tabular results displayed

steps.action: With destrieux engine selected, load 'Union of atlas regions' example query and click 'Run Query'
steps.expected: Results table displayed with column headers and at least one row of data
steps.observed: Results panel shows 'No results found' text despite backend returning successful results

steps.action: Verified via direct WebSocket test (parallel WS connection from browser eval)
steps.expected: Backend should return values in the WS response
steps.observed: Backend WS response for query 'LeftRegion(s, r) :- destrieux(s, r) & startswith("L", s)' returns: results={LeftRegion: {row_type: ['<class str>', '<class ExplicitVBR>'], columns: ['s', 'r'], size: 74, probabilistic: false, last_parsed_symbol: true}} — NO 'values' field in response

steps.expected: ResultsPanel should handle results without 'values' field
steps.observed: ResultsPanel filters results map with condition: 'values' in (val as object). Since backend WS response NEVER includes 'values' (QuerySocketHandler calls QueryResults(uuid, future) without get_values=True), the filter always fails and resultsMap is null, triggering 'No results found' state.

evidence.network: WebSocket ws://localhost:3100/v1/statementsocket -> done message without 'values' field

assertions.issues: ROOT CAUSE: Mismatch between backend and frontend. backend/app.py QuerySocketHandler.send_query_update() calls QueryResults(self.uuid, future) without get_values=True. The responses.py QueryResults class only includes 'values' when get_values=True. The frontend ResultsPanel.tsx (lines ~295-305) filters resultsMap using 'values' in (val as object) — this filter ALWAYS fails because the WS response never includes 'values'. Fix: either pass get_values=True in the QuerySocketHandler, OR update ResultsPanel to not require 'values' for presence check (use 'columns' instead). The size and column metadata ARE present in the backend response.

assertions.title: Symbol selector for results

steps.action: Load multi-symbol query (Union of atlas regions: LeftRegion, AnteriorOfCentralSulcus, SuperiorOfTemporalGyrus, RegionUnion) and run it
steps.expected: Symbol selector dropdown appears when multiple result symbols are available
steps.observed: Results panel shows 'No results found' — same root cause as VAL-EXEC-005. Backend returns 5 symbols (LeftRegion, AnteriorOfCentralSulcus, SuperiorOfTemporalGyrus, RegionUnion, destrieux) without 'values' field; ResultsPanel filters them out.

evidence.network: WebSocket WS response confirmed to include 5 symbols without 'values' field

assertions.issues: Blocked by same root cause as VAL-EXEC-005: ResultsPanel requires 'values' in result objects. Backend WS never includes 'values'. Symbol selector cannot appear because no results are rendered. The React queryResults state does contain the 5 symbols (LeftRegion, AnteriorOfCentralSulcus, SuperiorOfTemporalGyrus, RegionUnion, destrieux) confirming the data reaches the client — the frontend just discards them.

assertions.title: Sort results by column

steps.action: Attempted to execute query and view results table to test sorting
steps.expected: Clickable column headers that sort rows ascending/descending
steps.observed: No results table rendered — same root cause as VAL-EXEC-005

assertions.issues: Cannot test sort functionality because no results table is displayed. Blocked by same root cause as VAL-EXEC-005: backend WS response missing 'values' field.

assertions.title: Paginate results

steps.action: Attempted to run a query with >50 rows (destrieux has 148 total regions)
steps.expected: Pagination controls (Page 1 of N, prev/next) visible when more than 50 rows returned
steps.observed: No results table rendered — same root cause as VAL-EXEC-005

assertions.issues: Cannot test pagination because no results table is displayed. Backend returns size=148 for full destrieux relation in WS response, but frontend cannot render it due to missing 'values' field. Blocked by same root cause as VAL-EXEC-005.

assertions.title: Download results as CSV

steps.action: Attempted to execute query and locate CSV download button
steps.expected: CSV download button visible near results table; clicking it downloads a CSV file
steps.observed: No results table rendered, no CSV download button visible — same root cause as VAL-EXEC-005

assertions.issues: Cannot test CSV download because no results table is displayed. The DataTable component (which contains the 'Download CSV' button) is never rendered because resultsMap is null. Blocked by same root cause as VAL-EXEC-005. NOTE: The ResultsPanel.tsx code DOES implement CSV download functionality (downloadCSV function, symbolDataToCSV, '↓ Download CSV' button with data-testid='csv-download-btn') — the feature is implemented but untestable due to the backend/frontend 'values' field mismatch.

frictions.description: CodeMirror 6 editor does not respond to standard agent-browser keyboard commands (Control+A, Backspace, fill) for clearing content. The editor requires programmatic dispatch via the internal cmTile.view object or React context manipulation.
frictions.resolution: Used JavaScript eval to find the React QueryContext fiber and call setDatalogText() directly, or clicked example query buttons which properly update both CM state and React state.

frictions.description: Destrieux queries execute too fast (< 100ms) for agent-browser screenshot to capture the loading state at normal timing.
frictions.resolution: Injected a custom DelayedWS class that delays the WebSocket connection by 3 seconds, allowing screenshots to capture the 'Running...' + 'Cancel' button state before results arrive.

frictions.description: The keyboard type command in agent-browser appends text to CodeMirror editor instead of replacing existing content when using Control+A + type sequence.
frictions.resolution: Used example query buttons or React context setDatalogText() to set query content cleanly.

blockers.description: Backend/frontend mismatch: QuerySocketHandler.send_query_update() in app.py calls QueryResults(self.uuid, future) without get_values=True. The WS response therefore never includes the 'values' field. ResultsPanel.tsx requires 'values' in the result object to render any table (line ~295: `'values' in (val as object)`). This causes ALL result-dependent assertions to fail.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tested 5 different queries via direct WS from browser, all confirmed backend omits 'values'. Confirmed with source code inspection: responses.py only adds 'values' when get_values=True, and QuerySocketHandler never passes get_values=True. Fix required in app.py: change QueryResults(self.uuid, future) to QueryResults(self.uuid, future, get_values=True) OR update ResultsPanel.tsx to not require 'values' for filter check.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 1 passed (VAL-EXEC-004: loading state with spinner and cancel button clearly visible), 5 failed (VAL-EXEC-005 through VAL-EXEC-009: all blocked by a single root-cause bug — the backend WebSocket handler does not include row values in the query completion message, causing the frontend ResultsPanel to always show 'No results found'. The backend DOES execute queries correctly and returns 74-148 rows for destrieux queries as confirmed by direct WebSocket testing; the data simply never reaches the UI due to the missing 'values' field filter in ResultsPanel.tsx).
